danger_signals.visual_form: Creature sprites with distinct silhouettes and animations; demonic/skeletal designs
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat to player; engage or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Dungeon environments with fire, water, and other elemental visual effects
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental damage zones; requires careful navigation

danger_signals.visual_form: Dramatic architectural shifts, portal openings, lighting changes
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat level; final confrontation imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite damage overlays, HUD visual degradation (standard FPS convention)
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability; seek healing items immediately

safety_signals.visual_form: Central hub architecture with reduced enemy spawn rates, clear pathways
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity to regroup, plan, access side objectives

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating items with distinct visual signatures (health potions, mana, armor)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource acquisition; restoration of player capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Opened doors, cleared pathways, visual markers of level advancement
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage; puzzle solved or objective completed

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varied corrupted creature types serving Korax across different domains
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Necropolis, dark castle, corrupted dungeon aesthetics; necromantic visual language

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Class-specific visual identities: Fighter (armored warrior), Cleric (holy vestments), Mage (robes with magical aura)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Temples, seminaries, mountainside sanctuaries; light/holy architecture contrasts with Korax domains

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overrun landscapes; demonic corruption spreading through elemental dungeons, wilderness, and castle structures; necropolis as final bastion of dark power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies spawn increasingly in corrupted zones; environmental hazards intensify as player approaches Korax; creature behavior becomes more aggressive in late-game areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Central hub areas serve as visual waypoints; side levels branch visually distinct from hubs; polyobject-based puzzle elements (moving walls) create visual feedback of progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can traverse back-and-forth between hub and side missions; completing objectives unlocks new pathways; multi-step puzzles create environmental state changes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fighter displays heavier armor, larger physical frame; Cleric shows holy iconography; Mage exhibits magical aura and staff-focused design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each class moves at different speeds; weapon selection visually distinct; item functionality changes per-class (e.g., flechette behavior differs); combat approach varies by visual capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Moving walls (polyobjects) shift to reveal new paths; doors open with visual/audio cues; texture changes on sidedefs indicate state changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level scripting (ACS) triggers creature spawning, environmental effects (earthquakes), on-screen messages; multi-level puzzle states persist across hub transitions

environmental_storytelling.example: Elemental dungeons communicate fire/water/earth domains through visual design; mountainside seminary suggests holy resistance to corruption; necropolis at game's end signals final confrontation with death magic
Enemy encounters escalate in corrupted zones; lesser creatures in wilderness, greater demonic forces in castle and necropolis; boss arena isolation communicates finality
Central hub areas act as narrative anchor points; branch level accessibility communicates quest progression; visual locks (blocked pathways) indicate incomplete prerequisites
Moving walls reveal hidden areas and provide immediate visual confirmation of player actions; environmental shifts (floor height changes) caused by scripts communicate state changes to players

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fast movement, close-quarters melee attacks; visually robust stance; mundane and magical weapon switching
Medium movement speed; balanced melee/ranged attack capability; holy magic visual effects; middle-ground positioning
Slow movement; long-range spell casting with visual projectiles; fragile visual presentation; distance-dependent tactics
Sprite-based animation cycles; aggression increases on player detection; varied creature types show distinct attack patterns; behavior scripts tied to area state
Unique sprite design communicating ultimate threat; arena-specific attack patterns; visual dominance through scale and demonic appearance

notes: Hexen: Beyond Heretic communicates world-state primarily through environmental architecture, enemy creature design, and class-based visual differentiation. The hub system creates non-linear narrative pacing that is visually reinforced through pathway accessibility. Polyobject technology enables dynamic environmental feedback to player actions. The three-class system provides visual identity beyond gameplay mechanics, allowing players to recognize role-based limitations and capabilities at a glance. The progression from wilderness → castle → necropolis follows a visual corruption gradient, with Korax's domain intensifying visual demonic iconography. Limited faction identity exists (player heroes vs. Serpent Rider corruption), communicated through architectural contrast and creature design rather than explicit symbols. World-state changes are primarily scripted (ACS-based) with visual feedback through moving walls, texture shifts, and enemy spawning patterns.
